analyst_note: Q4 2025: New victim record (2,416 — surpassing Q4 2024's 1,663 and Q1 2025's 2,289). Full-year 2025: 7,500+ victims on leak sites (+58% YoY). Chainalysis 2025 annual payments: ~$820M (8% decline from 2024). Europol targeted The Com network (extortion/ransomware, Feb 2026 — likely Q4 2025 operational period). Coveware Q4 2025: payment rate 20% (new historic low). Median payment hit all-time high of $250K simultaneously — extreme divergence of mean vs. median.

a1._label: Russian Domestic Actions Against Ecosystem Actors
a1.notes: No documented RU domestic actions Q4 2025. Seven consecutive quarters of zero domestic enforcement. The sustained absence of any domestic action across this period constitutes a structural indicator of protection tolerance, not a data gap.

a2._label: Time Lag: Referral to Observable Russian Action

a3.notes: Populate from analyst model. This is the last historical quarter before the Q1 2026 baseline — protection relationship status here forms the comparative baseline.

events.name: Europol — The Com Network Arrests (Ransomware/Extortion)
events.notes: Europol targeted The Com network — English-speaking extortion/ransomware group with ties to Scattered Spider methodology. Arrests made. Score: 7 — mid-tier actors, not RU/CIS core, but network had significant operational scale. Note: announced Feb 2026, operations likely Q4 2025.

b1.notes: Europol The Com network operation represents continued LE focus on English-speaking affiliate tier. Full operational details pending — rescore if additional arrests or cooperation output confirmed. Q4 2025 WAIS may be revised upward when full details available.

b2.notes: Record victim count Q4 2025 driven by many small operators — ecosystem at maximum fragmentation to date. Exit scam confirmed (specific group TBD — update from analyst record). New RaaS brands at highest quarterly count. Forum activity elevated. Traditional RaaS model in structural decline per Chainalysis — affiliates rotating brands, reusing tooling. ANALYST INFERENCE — fragmentation confirmed by external reporting.

c1._label: Ransom Payment Volume vs. Victim Count
c1.source: Check Point Research Q4 2025 (2,416 victims — record); Chainalysis 2026 Crypto Crime Report (2025 annual ~$820M); Coveware Q4 2025
c1.notes: CREDIBILITY NOTE: Payment volume is ANALYST ESTIMATE from 2025 annual total (~$820M / 4 quarters adjusted for known H1 weighting). Victim count (2,416) is CONFIRMED from Check Point Research — new all-time record. Divergence at its most extreme: record attack volume + record non-payment. Chainalysis notes 2025 full-year payments ~8% below 2024.

c2.source: Coveware Q4 2025 quarterly report (overall rate: 20% — new historic low)
c2.notes: Coveware Q4 2025: overall payment rate 20% (new historic low). Median payment: all-time high of $250,000 simultaneously — extreme bifurcation. Many victims not paying; those who do pay substantially more. Populate sector breakdown from Coveware Q4 2025 report.

c3.source: Check Point Research Q4 2025 (2,416 — record); ransomware.live 2025 annual stats
c3.notes: Record Q4 victim count. No major LE-initiated takedowns of primary leak sites in Q4 2025. Full-year 2025: 7,500+ victims on leak sites (+58% YoY from ~4,750 in 2024). Small operator fragmentation drives record numbers — 47+ groups with <10 victims each contributing to aggregate. Q4 2025 establishes the pre-baseline comparison point for Q1 2026.
